key_insight: A sparse random projection plus multinomial logistic regression model improved DNA methylation-based CNS tumor classification over the reference approach.
clinical_relevance: Better classification accuracy could improve tumor subtype assignment and support more reliable downstream treatment decisions.
method: Reduced high-dimensional methylation features with Sparse Random Projection.
Used multinomial logistic regression for multiclass tumor classification.
Benchmarked performance with stratified cross-validation and an independent clinical cohort.
evidence_signal: The model reached 96% mean accuracy in 3-fold cross-validation on 2,801 reference samples and 86% class-level accuracy on 1,104 independent clinical samples, rising to 93% at the family level.
watch_item: Performance was reported on specific cohorts, so external validation across additional sites and platforms is still important before broad implementation.
paper_title: A Novel Machine Learning Approach for Central Nervous System Tumor Classification from DNA Methylation
